mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising, discount rates and funding costs tighten simultaneously, pressuring equity valuations and short-horizon S&P 500 returns. The effect should be strongest over the next 20 trading days and should flip weaker once policy stops rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in most periods when FEDFUNDS has risen on a 3-month basis, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in a rolling 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening household income and demand, which should translate into lower S&P 500 returns over the next month as analysts mark down earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with a 0.3 pp three-month UNRATE rise do not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail demand, supporting earnings momentum and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day S&P 500 returns are not concentrated in months where both PAYEMS and RSAFS are improving together, or if either series alone explains the signal better than the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial activity and cyclical growth, which should support equities with a lag and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not higher following sustained INDPRO improvements, or if the signal reverses sign during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy rates tighten and equity multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates for 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if elevated inflation combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% does not precede weaker 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the joint condition adds no predictive power beyond CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 relative to prior quarters signals broad demand weakening and falling earnings prospects, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than baseline, or if the relationship disappears after controlling for contemporaneous labor data.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS indicates a still-tight but improving labor market, which supports consumer spending and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the labor backdrop is firming.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5% and PAYEMS rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-UNRATE and rising-PAYEMS condition does not precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the effect vanishes when either proxy is removed.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST signals housing-led demand reacceleration and a stronger cyclical outlook, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month as cyclicals and earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 8% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts do not lead to above-average positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal is absent after repeated threshold crossings.
